---
title: "Introductory Course — Setup Guide"
subtitle: "Pre-session checklist for facilitators"
---

## Purpose

This guide ensures every resident arrives at Week 1 with a working development environment. Do not assume prior technical knowledge. Test this process yourself on a clean machine before the cohort begins.

---

## OS Requirements

| OS | Minimum Version | Notes |
|----|-----------------|-------|
| Windows | Windows 10 | WSL2 recommended for Git Bash compatibility |
| macOS | macOS 11 (Big Sur) | Terminal.app or iTerm2 |
| Linux | Ubuntu 20.04 LTS | Any modern distro with `apt` equivalent |

---

## Installation Steps

### 1. R (4.3.0 or newer)

**Windows:**
1. Download from [cran.r-project.org](https://cran.r-project.org/bin/windows/base/)
2. Run installer with default options
3. Verify: Open RGui, type `R.version.string`

**macOS:**
```bash
brew install --cask r
```

> **Note:** The `--cask` flag installs the official CRAN R with full toolchain support needed for package compilation.

**Linux (Ubuntu):**
```bash
sudo apt update
sudo apt install r-base
```

### 2. RStudio or VSCode

**RStudio (recommended for beginners):**
- Download from [posit.co/download/rstudio-desktop/](https://posit.co/download/rstudio-desktop/)

**VSCode (alternative):**
- Download from [code.visualstudio.com](https://code.visualstudio.com/)
- Install extensions: R Extension Pack, Quarto

### 3. Quarto

Download from [quarto.org/docs/get-started](https://quarto.org/docs/get-started/)

Verify installation:
```bash
quarto --version
```

### 4. Git

**Windows:**
- Download from [git-scm.com](https://git-scm.com/download/win)
- Use Git Bash as terminal

**macOS:**
```bash
xcode-select --install
```

**Linux:**
```bash
sudo apt install git
```

Verify:
```bash
git --version
```

### 5. GitHub Account

1. Create account at [github.com](https://github.com)
2. Enable two-factor authentication
3. Add SSH key (follow GitHub docs)
4. Test: `ssh -T git@github.com`

---

## Pre-Session Distribution

Send residents this checklist **2 weeks before Week 1**:

```
Subject: Medical Data Science — Pre-Course Setup Required

Dear Resident,

Before our first session, please complete the environment setup:

□ Install R (4.3.0+): https://cran.r-project.org/
□ Install RStudio: https://posit.co/download/rstudio-desktop/
□ Install Quarto: https://quarto.org/docs/get-started/
□ Install Git: https://git-scm.com/downloads
□ Create GitHub account: https://github.com (enable 2FA)
□ Add SSH key to GitHub (follow their documentation)

Verification: Can you run these commands in Terminal/Git Bash?
  R --version        (Should show R version 4.3.0 or newer)
  git --version
  quarto --version
  ssh -T git@github.com

Reply to this email with "Setup complete" once done.
```

---

## Troubleshooting Common Issues

| Issue | Resolution |
|-------|------------|
| "R not found" | Check PATH; restart RStudio after R installation |
| Git Bash won't open | Install Git with "Use Git from Git Bash only" option |
| SSH key rejected | Ensure key is added to ssh-agent: `eval "$(ssh-agent -s)" && ssh-add ~/.ssh/id_ed25519` |
| Quarto render fails | Check for spaces in file paths (Windows) |

---

## Facilitator Verification

Before the cohort starts:

- [ ] Tested setup on clean Windows VM
- [ ] Tested setup on clean macOS install
- [ ] Sent pre-session email to all residents
- [ ] Tracked responses; followed up with non-responders
- [ ] Scheduled 1-on-1 sessions for residents reporting issues
